author: y4rr
content: Surfaced during Phase 9 (#1010) value-parity attempt. ferrotorch deeplabv3.rs:280-303 ResNet50Dilated::new builds layer3 with uniform dilation=2 across all 6 DilatedBottleneck blocks and layer4 with uniform dilation=4 across all 3 blocks (deeplabv3.rs:466-484 make_dilated_layer is the offender). torchvision's deeplabv3_resnet50 (replace_stride_with_dilation=[False, True, True]) goes through ResNet._make_layer which uses 'previous_dilation' for the FIRST block and the new (×stride) dilation for subsequent blocks: layer3[0]=1, layer3[1..]=2, layer4[0]=2, layer4[1..]=4. Verified live via Python torchvision inspection. Symptoms: deeplabv3_resnet50_value_parity fails element-0 allclose with max_abs=9.085e-2 vs tol=9.336e-3 (~10x out of band) even with all weight tensors loaded byte-equivalent and 4/5 honest-fail probes passing. FCN-ResNet50 (also replace_stride_with_dilation=[False, True, True]) PASSes value parity because it uses resnet.rs::resnet50_dilated, whose make_bottleneck_layer correctly threads current_dilation. The hand-rolled make_dilated_layer in deeplabv3.rs:466-484 was an artefact of Phase 6 #988 (ASPP migration) that didn't get unified with the resnet.rs path. Fix: rewrite ResNet50Dilated::new to either (a) reuse resnet.rs::resnet50_dilated directly or (b) replicate make_bottleneck_layer's previous_dilation threading. Option (a) is preferable but requires resolving the divergence between deeplabv3.rs DilatedBottleneck (which uses DilatedConv2d wrapping conv+BN+ReLU as one module, exposing 'conv2.bn.<...>') and resnet.rs Bottleneck (which uses raw Conv2d with explicit dilation, exposing 'bn2.<...>'). Tracking issue: #1010 Phase 9. Closes blocker for 13/14 PASS-able vision models.
